items.title: Verbannen Smeets haalt uit naar MVV
items.summary: Bryan Smeets kwam dit seizoen niet in actie voor MVV Maastricht, omdat hij is verbannen naar het beloftenelftal. Daar traint hij en speelt hij soms een oefenwedstrijd, maar meer zit er niet in voor de 33-jarige. In gesprek met ESPN haalt Smeets uit naar MVV.

items.title: FIFPRO krijgt stemrecht bij UEFA: overbelasting spelers hoog op de agenda
items.summary: De UEFA heeft aangekondigd dat de FIFPRO stemrecht krijgt binnen het Uitvoerende Comité van het Europese voetbalorgaan. Dat is een mijlpaal, want deze wereldwijde vakbond zet zich in voor de arbeidsomstandigheden van spelers. Onder meer de overbelasting van voetballers staat als een van de hoogste prioriteiten op de agenda.

items.title: How Liverpool transformed their corner threat
items.summary: Arne Slot welcomed Liverpool's set-piece efficiency in their 1-0 win over Sunderland at the Stadium of Light. "Usually we concede one, but tonight we could score and that's the difference for us in this game," he said in his press conference.

items.title: Oranje kiest Kansas City als uitvalsbasis tijdens WK
items.summary: Het Nederlands elftal verblijft tijdens het WK van komende zomer in Kansas. Dat heeft de KNVB donderdag bekendgemaakt. De ploeg van bondscoach Ronald Koeman gaat trainen op het complex van Kansas City Current, dat uitkomt in de National Women's Soccer League (NWSL).

items.title: Spurs' Odobert to undergo surgery after rupturing ACL
items.summary: Tottenham winger Wilson Odobert will undergo surgery after suffering a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament in his left knee.

items.title: Pereira agrees to replace Dyche at Forest
items.summary: Vitor Pereira has agreed to become the next Nottingham Forest head coach.

items.title: Ange: Frank sacking 'fair,' but Spurs lack vision
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has labelled Thomas Frank's sacking a "fair departure" but sympathised with the Dane for having to work in an "environment of uncertainty."

items.title: Wrexham's Parkinson welcomes expanded playoffs
items.summary: Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson has welcomed the prospect of an expanded play-off system in the Championship, insisting there is no shortage of Premier League potential in the division.

items.title: FA: Tuchel's new deal not related to United job
items.summary: Football Association chief executive Mark Bullingham said there are "performance conditions" connected to Thomas Tuchel staying on as England head coach beyond the World Cup.

items.title: Dyche out as Forest sack 3rd coach this season
items.summary: Nottingham Forest sacked coach Sean Dyche on Wednesday, following his team's 0-0 draw with Premier League bottom-club Wolves, the team announced.

items.title: 'Bayern en Chelsea strijden om Verbruggen, De Zerbi kandidaat bij Spurs'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Voetbaltop eensgezind op UEFA-congres: 'Het voetbal heeft gewonnen'
items.summary: Tijdens het UEFA-congres is in Brussel opgetogen gereageerd op het einde van de Super League. Woensdag werd bekend dat Real Madrid tot een akkoord was gekomen met de UEFA en de European Football Clubs (EFC), waarmee de beoogde nieuwe competitie definitief tot het verleden behoort en ook alle juridische geschillen zijn beëindigd.

items.title: Maswanhise warns 'watch out for us' in title race as Motherwell future addressed
items.summary: Tawanda Maswanhise has warned Hearts, Celtic and Rangers that Motherwell are still in the title race.

items.title: Title race ON! The DRAMATIC late goals shaking up the Scottish Premiership!
items.summary: A dramatic round of fixtures saw late winners from Celtic and Hearts, while Rangers dropped points at Motherwell!

items.title: Nottingham Forest close in on Pereira appointment
items.summary: Nottingham Forest close to appointing former Wolves boss Vitor Pereira as their new head coach.

items.title: QPR and Crawley sued for £11.1m by former player who claims they did not protect him
items.summary: Amrit Bansal-McNulty claim relates to alleged racism Clubs deny wrongdoing in case being heard at tribunal Queens Park Rangers and Crawley Town are being sued for more than £11m by a former player who is claiming they failed to protect him from alleged racist banter that he says destroyed his career. The Championship club are joint defendants with Crawley in a claim currently being heard at the Central London Employment Tribunal, brought by the former Northern Ireland Under-21 international Amrit Bansal-McNulty, who is suing for £11.1m for a loss of opportunity as a footballer and personal injury. Both clubs have denied wrongdoing. Continue reading...

items.title: From hope to despair: Nottingham Forest's season to forget
items.summary: This season was supposed to be the one Nottingham Forest become entrenched in the European positions. Instead, they’re in a relegation scrap and are moving on to their fourth coach.

items.title: Did Dyche deserve the sack at Nottingham Forest?
items.summary: After Sean Dyche's reign lasts just 114 days, Nottingham Forest are on the hunt for a fourth head coach of the season - but as he hard done by?

items.title: Bring on the old guard to beat the drop: can Ange’s recall be right twist for Spurs? | Max Rushden
items.summary: If Tottenham are waiting for Pochettino part two, then season three of Postecoglou might bring the right survival vibes It’s panic time at the bottom of the Premier League and, if the past couple of days are anything to go by, probably don’t go following Ange Postecoglou into a job any time soon. Others who’ve followed it more closely can do Nottingham Forest and their 4 (four) managers. This is a piece about Tottenham Hotspur, or as I like to call them, my big team who win things. November 2023 feels like a lifetime ago. Spurs were top of the league. Angeball was at its peak. Dynamic free-flowing football – they were 1-0 up against Chelsea thanks to Dejan Kulusevski (injured). It’s the 14th minute, Spurs neatly play themselves out from the back down the right, it breaks to Pape Sarr who rolls the ball to Destiny Udogie (injured), and Brennan Johnson (Crystal Palace) steams down the left. He plays a perfect first-time ball with his left foot into the path of Son Heung-min (LAFC), who rolls it home. Tottenham are 2-0 up against a team they lose to at least twice a season. Continue reading...

items.title: Tottenham are 'not a big club' - Postecoglou
items.summary: Former head coach Ange Postecoglou criticises Tottenham's transfer strategy in the wake of Thomas Frank's sacking.
